description: §6.6.9 E-REACTIVE-003 (RULING A) — a WHOLLY server-escalated function reading a `const <name>` derived is a compile error. `saveOrder()` escalates (it touches a `?{}` SQL resource, §12) and reads the client-side `const <total>` derived; on the server that read lowers to `_scrml_body["total"]`, but the fn has NO reactive assignment so it is NOT CPS-split — its client stub sends only declared params, so `@total` is never transported and resolves to `undefined` server-side. The compiler rejects the read. Fix: pass `@total` as an explicit argument (args ARE marshalled) or declare a §52 `<... server>` cell. Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 / W-SQL-ROW-UNTYPED ignored by superset.
rationale: §6.6.9: 'A WHOLLY server-escalated function body that READS a client cell — a mutable @variable OR a const <name> derived — SHALL be a compile error, E-REACTIVE-003.' The value is not transported (`_scrml_body["total"]` is never populated by a non-CPS stub), so the read is rejected rather than silently ship an undefined-valued query.
